rulings.Case Title: LEVALTON BROWN et al VS. EDWARD LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Cont. Action After Death Of One Of The Persons Who Brought The Action And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint To Include A Wrongful Death Claim And To Remove Case From The Trial Calendar
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 8, 2010, line 4, PLAINTIFF MARY BROWN Motion To Continue Action After Death Of One Of The Persons Who Brought The Action and For Leave To File First Amended Complaint To Include a Wrongful Death Claim. Motion to substitute widow as successor-in-interest and to file first amended complaint is granted, no opposition filed. Parties referred to Department 206 to address the trial date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IAIN BAKER VS. CHARTER COMMUNICATIONS HOLDING CO., LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 8, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT FALCON CABLE SYSTEMS COMPANY II, L.P., GARY PROCTOR Motion to Transfer Venue. Motion denied. Defendant fails to overcome the presumption that venue is proper in the county where plaintiff filed the claim. There is no evidence to support a discretionary transfer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LEO GIRALDI VS. CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Class Action Pursuant To Crc 3.770
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 8, 2010, line 8, PLAINTIFF LEO GIRALDI Motion To Dismiss Class Action Pursuant To CRC 3.770. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MACMILLAN VS. WILLIAM MAYTA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 8, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend to plead discovery of wrong doing with more particularity and to plead defendant's mispresentations and/or a cause of action for concealment/deceit. Plaintiff must allege facts to support alter ego liability, plaintiff's contract was with the corporation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MACMILLAN VS. WILLIAM MAYTA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 8, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MAYTA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead a basis for attorney fees and there are no facts that show the tort of another doctrine applies. If plaintiff cures the errors cited in the demurrer, then plaintiff's claims for punitive damages and 10% interest may be proper. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. DAVID C WILLIS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 8, 2010, line 14, PLAINTIFFS JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Triable issue of fact created whether plaintiff complied with Civil Code section 2923.5. No declaration attached to notice of defect. =(302/CWW)
